prompt: Write a short poem (8–16 lines) about the feeling of finishing a long project. Any form you like.
response:
**"The Last Stroke"**

The pen lifts—
a held breath released at last.
The page, once a wilderness,
now a map with creases worn smooth.

No more the weight of half-done things,
no more the ghost of what might be—
only this: the quiet hum
of a door clicked shut behind you.

The light is different now.
The air tastes like the first sip
of water after thirst.
You are empty. You are full.

The world, suddenly wide,
asks nothing of you.
You laugh—
then begin again.
